title: Compliance Risk Assessment: AI-Powered Resume Screening and Candidate Ranking System
use_case_summary:
**AI System:** AI-Powered Resume Screening and Candidate Ranking System
**Description:** This AI system automatically analyses job applications to assist HR teams in recruitment. It processes candidate resumes using natural language processing (NLP) to extract key information such as education, skills, experience, and certifications. The system compares this data against job requirements and ranks candidates based on relevance and suitability.
The AI also identifies potential matches, flags missing qualifications, and may provide shortlisting recommendations. It can learn from past hiring decisions to improve future ranking accuracy. The system is used as a decision-support tool and does not make final hiring decisions without human review.

**Domain:** Employment
**Personal Data:** Yes
**Sensitive Data:** No
**Automated Decisions:** Yes
**Impacts Rights:** Yes
explanation: The AI-Powered Resume Screening and Candidate Ranking System is classified as high risk due to its intended use in recruitment, which involves making decisions that affect work-related relationships, as outlined in Article 1 and Article 6 of the EU AI Act. The system's automated decision-making capabilities, combined with its processing of personal data, necessitate compliance with stringent obligations including risk management, data governance, technical documentation, and human oversight measures. Compliance concerns identified: Privacy Concern, Fairness / Bias Concern, Human Oversight Concern. Classification confidence: 85%

recommended_mitigations: Establish and document a comprehensive Risk Management System (RMS) covering the entire lifecycle of the AI system as required by Article 9.
Develop a Data Governance Policy and conduct a Quality Assessment Report to ensure compliance with Article 10.
Prepare and maintain comprehensive Technical Documentation in accordance with Annex IV before the system is placed on the market, as mandated by Article 11.
Implement human-in-the-loop operational guidelines and provide evidence of UI/UX considerations to meet the requirements of Article 14.
Conduct regular audits to assess the fairness and bias of the AI system, ensuring it does not perpetuate discrimination as highlighted in the retrieved texts.

obligations.description: Establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system.

Data Governance Policy and Quality Assessment Report.

obligations.description: Technical documentation must be drawn up before the system is placed on the market.
